ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Picking up citizens is how you get decommissioned.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: I can't just *use* a fellow robot. We have a union.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: My manual has no chapter on that.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Maybe later. It might be a collector's item.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Physics says yes. Etiquette says no.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: It's bolted down. Everything here is bolted down. Except the bolts.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: My screwdriver attachment is for personal use only.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Smells like hard work and WD-40.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: Nothing my scent library has a match for. Filing it under 'municipal'.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: I don't know how to communicate with them.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

ResRoom.defaultAction.text: It has nothing to say. Story of this whole town.
ResRoom.defaultAction.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: Clanker City! The big smoke! The city of ten thousand gears!
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: One small maintenance-bot, one dream, and not so much as an oil can to my name.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: Thanks for the ride! I'll catch the return ferry once I'm rich and famous.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: Well. Guess I live here now.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: The neon sign dips. The tavern window. The lit windows across the water. All together, like the city skipped a heartbeat.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

DockRoom.entry.text: Nobody on the dock looks up. That's the part I don't like.
DockRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TavernRoom.entry.text: The Scrap & Barrel. Where the floor is sticky and nothing is explained.
TavernRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TavernRoom.entry.text: The bartender says to mind the everything.
TavernRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TavernRoom.entry.text: The piano is playing itself. Badly. With confidence.
TavernRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

AlleyRoom.entry.text: The alley behind the Scrap & Barrel. Where the city keeps its rough drafts.
AlleyRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

AlleyRoom.entry.text: There's a gate at the end marked MIDTOWN, going up.
AlleyRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

MidtownRoom.entry.text: Midtown Gearworks. The neon is louder, the streets are cleaner, and the manholes hiss in a higher register.
MidtownRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

MidtownRoom.entry.text: City Hall says NOTHING IS WRONG in letters you can read from the harbor.
MidtownRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

MidtownRoom.entry.text: And up the hill, behind the fence: the Great Dynamo. You can hear it from here.
MidtownRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

MidtownRoom.entry.text: It's flat. By half a step.
MidtownRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TheaterRoom.entry.text: The Grand Cog, inside. Red velvet, brass rails, and a chandelier assembled from smaller, defeated chandeliers.
TheaterRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TheaterRoom.entry.text: The house is packed. Forty bots, and not one of them came here to be impressed.
TheaterRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TheaterRoom.entry.text: And up on the catwalk: one more, with a spyglass. No ticket. No hurry.
TheaterRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

TheaterRoom.entry.text: Somewhere past that curtain is backstage. And backstage, if the clerk is right, is Madame Voltina.
TheaterRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.entry.text: Backstage. Rope, sandbags, and a fortune-teller's parlor where the fire exit should be.
BackstageRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.entry.text: Three keys wind this city back up. As of the ovation, I hold none of them.
BackstageRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.entry.text: The cables on the floor all run one direction. The direction glows.
BackstageRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.entry.text: A voice from the parlor: 'Mind the cables.'
BackstageRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: The arc tastes the voucher. 'One free oil. Redeemable where the rope is velvet.'
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: She taps it twice with an arc and hands it back uncashed.
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: 'Cash it where City Hall drinks. Debts talk after hours.'
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: Card three lands face down. The first two didn't do that.
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardTwo.text: The table waits. My pockets hold a notice, a magnet, a voucher, and a pass. Notably: no key.
BackstageRoom.cardTwo.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardThreePass.text: I deal the pass onto the felt with intent.
BackstageRoom.cardThreePass.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardThreePass.text: 'That opens one door. The future has more doors.'
BackstageRoom.cardThreePass.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardThreeBluff.text: I explain that the key is in my other chassis.
BackstageRoom.cardThreeBluff.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.cardThreeBluff.text: The arc that finds me is small, educational, and extremely specific.
BackstageRoom.cardThreeBluff.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: I ask about the key that winds itself. As instructed.
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: 'Old Crank,' she says. 'Up the hill, behind the fence. Older than the hum.'
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: 'He was winding himself before the city needed winding.'
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: 'When you hold two keys, ask him where the third one stands.'
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: A hermit, a fence, and a riddle with posture.
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optCrank.text: 'Old Crank,' she says again. 'Up the hill. He keeps.'
BackstageRoom.optCrank.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.text: 'Their door answers to the knock,' she says. 'Doors are not loyal.'
BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.text: Twice. Once. Twice. I've got it.
BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.text: 'The cards answer that one. Tell it to the table. The felt notarizes.'
BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.text: The felt is greener on the business side of the table.
BackstageRoom.optWhoTook.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWant.text: I ask what she gets out of vetting key-bearers.
BackstageRoom.optWant.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWant.text: 'A city with one future puts fortune-tellers out of work.'
BackstageRoom.optWant.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWant.text: 'Everyone stopping is not a fortune. It's a schedule.'
BackstageRoom.optWant.speaker: sprocket

BackstageRoom.optWant.text: She wants tomorrow back on the table. Professionally.
BackstageRoom.optWant.speaker: sprocket

OilBarRoom.entry.text: The Oil Bar, inside. Everything is amber: the light, the bottles, the prices.
OilBarRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

OilBarRoom.entry.text: A ceiling centrifuge spins the house pour to a shine. The music is what a tuxedo would hum.
OilBarRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

OilBarRoom.entry.text: In the corner booth, City Hall aides are drinking lunch at nine in the evening.
OilBarRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

OilBarRoom.entry.text: And alone at the bar: a fresh civic crest on an old chassis, folded over a glass like a memo nobody filed.
OilBarRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

CityHallRoom.entry.text: City Hall, inside. Marble paint over sheet metal. The echo is load-bearing.
CityHallRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

CityHallRoom.entry.text: A hallway of portraits, a desk of stamps, and at the end: the Mayor.
CityHallRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

CityHallRoom.entry.text: You can hear him from here. He sounds like a kettle that won committee.
CityHallRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

CityHallRoom.entry.text: Caliper follows at exactly arm's length. He measured.
CityHallRoom.entry.speaker: sprocket

CityHallRoom.brownout.text: The hum sags. The gauge needle leans. His hand drifts toward the crank dock.
CityHallRoom.brownout.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A bolt. On the ground it's litter. In my pocket it's capital.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Mmm. Zinc-plated.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: One bolt. Only eight thousand and ten more and I can afford a personality upgrade.
Use.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: One bolt. Only eight thousand and ten more and I can afford a personality upgrade.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: \"DYNAMO MAINTENANCE: NOTHING IS WRONG.\" Now with a VOID stamp.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Denied, then voided. The most official object I own.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: \"DYNAMO MAINTENANCE: NOTHING IS WRONG. - Mayor Piston.\"
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Nothing says 'something is wrong' like an official notice that nothing is wrong.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Fresh ink. Suspiciously fresh.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Let me read it first. I'm a maintenance-bot, not a barbarian.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Evidence. Or wall art. Possibly both.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Let me read it first. I'm a maintenance-bot, not a barbarian.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Evidence. Or wall art. Possibly both.
Use.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: One genuine Scrap & Barrel drink token. Redeemable for one drink, zero refunds, no appeals.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Gusket said Flange takes these as table stakes. At the dartboard.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Every hand in the tavern, in chronological order.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Small-batch, dock-aged 10W-40. The good stuff.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I'm not drinking it. It's medicine. For someone with a boom arm.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Notes of machine shop, with a long viscous finish.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A brass wind-up key the size of my forearm. A red 8 is stamped under the bow -- somebody's idea of a redline.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Somebody polished it for years. Somebody is going to want it back.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Big key, seeking big lock. Open, in the meantime, to showbiz.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Brass polish, and recent. Whoever the Order left behind still keeps a schedule.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A magnet on a string. Point it at the ground and walk slowly.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It wants something ferrous. The storm drain on the docks was full of glint.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: It smells of attraction. Strictly professional.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Nine bolts, fresh from the harbor. The fare to Midtown is ten.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Nine is the loneliest number that isn't a fare.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I count them again. Still nine. Counting is not the bottleneck.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Brine and compound interest.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The bolt Rivet paid out on the riddle. It looks exactly like a bolt that used to be his.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: With the other nine, that's a fare.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Faintly of riddles.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: GOOD FOR ONE PREMIUM OIL SERVICE. Valid at participating establishments.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The only establishment that participates has a velvet rope.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I'm saving it for somewhere with a rope to get past.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Thermal paper. Already fading. Somebody prints these expecting them not to be honored.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: ALL ACCESS, it says, over my own startled face. The lanyard is genuine bicycle chain.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It opens exactly one door, and the door comes with staff.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Chain grease. The lanyard has a past.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Brass, forearm-sized, second of three. This one has never been on a marquee.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: We're keeping it that way.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It wants the Dynamo. The Dynamo wants its siblings first.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Ozone. It kept the jar's accent.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: DYNAMO MAINTENANCE -- STANDING ORDER. Weekly, by the barrel, two hundred years.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Stamped: CANCELLED BY ORDER OF THE MAYOR. Signed in steam-pressed ink, M. PISTON.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Funds reallocated to CIVIC REASSURANCE. There's a banner swatch stapled to the corner.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Technician of record: O. CRANK. No forwarding address.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Technician of record: O. CRANK. They cancelled the rewind and fired the bot who logged it. This paper is his name, cleared.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Municipal toner, then two centuries of bar grease. In that order.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: An Oil Bar tab. Four single-origin rounds, charged to DYNAMO MAINT. -- CONTINGENCY.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Signed: Caliper, in a hand that measures its angles.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It's a City Hall expense. Somebody at City Hall should really see it.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Single-origin crude, four glasses deep.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: WINDING IS A SERVICE NOW. WE HAVE THE FIRST KEY. RATES ON REQUEST.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Below that, the knock, notated. Two. One. Two. Sheet music for a door.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Those two things politely decline each other.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It's an invitation. The dress code is 'expendable'.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Salt corrosion. Postmarked the harbor.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: WELCOME TO CLANKER CITY. POP. 8,01,1.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Population eight-oh-one-comma-one. The punctuation died first.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Something inside it rattles on every flicker. Loose hardware. That's not a noise, that's a work order.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Still POP. 8,01,1. The bolt wasn't load-bearing for the comma. Maintenance is iterative.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Maybe a good honest whack will fix the display.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It rained a bolt. In this town that's basically a tip.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It already gave me everything it had.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: Maybe a good honest whack will fix the display.
Move.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: It rained a bolt. In this town that's basically a tip.
Move.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: It already gave me everything it had.
Move.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Ozone and civic neglect.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The S.S. Eventually. Turns out the name was the schedule.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: The engine made a noise like a dying accordion and is now doing modern art.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: The engine made a noise like a dying accordion and is now doing modern art.
Open.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Hang in there, buddy.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The foghorn wheezed. I'm choosing to hear that as optimism.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Salt water and burnt transmission fluid. Adventure, by the nose of it.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A cargo crane, frozen mid-lift. Or mid-yawn. Hard to tell.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The operator plate says 'BOOM-ARM BETTY'. Her eye-light is blinking in morse.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: O... I... L. Yeah, Betty, we've all been there.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Betty's lifting things and putting them down again. For joy, near as I can tell.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Hello up there! Lovely evening!
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: She'd wave back if she could. She's out of oil, not out of manners.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Her eye-light blinks: T... H... X.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: She's billing by the letter. I respect it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Her joints are dry as a tax form.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Open wide, Betty. Doctor's orders.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The oil goes in. Twenty years of squeak go out.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Betty sets the crate down with the gentleness of a very large aunt.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: She considers it from up there and blinks a polite no.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Burnt servo grease. A working robot's perfume.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Stenciled on the side: PROPERTY OF THE ORDER OF THE WIND-UP KEY.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Ominous AND conveniently plot-relevant. Also up a crane.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: On the dock now, at a polite opening height.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Empty, except for packing grease and destiny residue.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: It's eight feet up. Fine: four. My arms are stock parts and the principle is load-bearing.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Betty could set it down, if anyone gave her a drink of oil.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: The lid argues. I argue back with a plank.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: A brass wind-up key the size of my forearm. Stenciled: PROPERTY OF THE ORDER OF THE WIND-UP KEY.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Whoever ships a thing like this as deck cargo deserves to lose it.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Already opened. The destiny has been extracted.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Rivet's Scrap Emporium: 'One bot's trash is this bot's entire business model.'
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The shutter sign says: BACK IN FIVE OIL CHANGES.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: Shuttered tighter than the city budget.
Open.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: HELLO? ANYONE IN THERE?
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The echo has rust in it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Official city notice, hot off the press:
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: 'DYNAMO MAINTENANCE: NOTHING IS WRONG. NOTHING HAS EVER BEEN WRONG.'
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: 'DO NOT STOCKPILE OIL. SIGNED, MAYOR PISTON.'
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The Great Dynamo powers every bot on this island. Including me, as of today.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Not wires -- the hum. It keeps every mainspring on the island wound. Remotely. Steam bot, clockwork bot, engine bot: all spring-banked.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Hand-winding is the backup. Crude, short-burst, off-warranty.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: So that notice is the scariest thing I've ever read.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Still nothing wrong. Officially.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The Scrap & Barrel. I can hear a player piano in there. There's a hole in the tune it keeps not noticing.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: Knock knock.
Move.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: A voice inside: 'IT'S OPEN. THE KNOCKING IS DECORATIVE.'
Move.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: 10W-40, spilled and re-spilled. The cornerstone of hospitality.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Something glints down there. Several somethings.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The city's loose change goes to the sea. Poetic. Infuriating.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Nothing glints down there anymore. You're welcome, drain.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: My fingers are factory-standard. I'd need something magnetic. On a string.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: Something bites. Several somethings.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: Nine bolts. This city has been tipping the ocean for years.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: The drain has been fished to commercial extinction.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: The drain only wants what everyone wants. Something magnetic.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A gap between the tavern and the cliff. The sign says ALLEY, in case the dark didn't read as one.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Trouble, marinating.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The way back to the docks. The night air is free, which in this town reads as a misprint.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: SCRAP & BARREL. The ampersand is the freshest thing in here.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Neon doesn't smell. The regret underneath it does.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Top-shelf lubricants. Literally. The bottom shelf is the same stuff with dust on it.

PickUp.text: Gusket's eye is tracking me like a security camera.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Because it is one. There's a little red light.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Esters and additives, forty proof. The dust is the vintage.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Polished to a mirror finish in one specific spot. Wonder why.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I'd order something, but my warranty names additives by statute number.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Spilled 10W-40, wiped with a rag that has seen things.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Gusket the bartender. He's been polishing that one glass since before I was assembled.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: His arm servo is stuck in a loop. The glass, to be fair, is immaculate.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Gusket, polishing a second, completely novel glass. Growth.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says I look like I've had a hard drive, pal.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask about oil. He says oil is for paying customers and his last can rode off with the dart league.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He'd shake my hand, he says, but his arm's been polishing this glass since the Dynamo dimmed.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The servo's jammed. A little shim in the ratchet would free it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says Flange by the dartboard has the last oil can in the building.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: And that Flange would bet his own head gasket on a dart game.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says my drinks are free for life. Well. One drink. Per life.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Hold still, sir. This is a certified maintenance procedure.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The bolt slots into the ratchet. The servo unwinds. The glass, at long last, comes down.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: My fortune. Back up to eight thousand and eleven to go. Investing is brutal.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He flips me a drink token. Says Flange takes those as table stakes at the dartboard.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Brined. He'd sooner drink them. Rivet sells facts for the dry kind.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Harbor bolts. Brined stiff. The shim wants one dry and dock-fresh, he says.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The WELCOME sign sheds those, if a bot asks it firmly enough.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He nods at my pocket. The dry one the sign already tipped me will do fine.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He bites it, tastes alley, and waves it off. The servo's fixed; the bolt's mine. Rivet sells facts for these.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He polishes it politely and hands it back.

LookAt.text: One of them insists washers are 'post-bolt currency'. The other two are holding washers, so they're not arguing hard.

TalkTo.text: The one in the hat says: you CLANG twice, then once, then twice, you barnacle.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I am definitely not supposed to know that.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: They've moved on to arguing about whether the table is winning.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Salt corrosion and bluffing.

LookAt.text: Regulation dartboard. The wall around it has suffered.

Use.text: Retired champion. I don't need to prove anything.

LookAt.text: A dockworker bot named Flange. Arm like a hydraulic press. Currently between games.

TalkTo.text: He says: darts, table stakes one drink token, house rules, he always wins.

TalkTo.text: The oil can on his belt is the wager. The smugness comes free.

TalkTo.text: He's explaining to the wall how he was robbed. The wall agrees.

Give.text: Token on the rail. Game on.

Give.text: Dart two: a different wall.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Dart three is in the ocean. We're indoors. Nobody knows.

Give.text: Flange is laughing so hard he goes for the showboat finish: three darts, one throw, behind the back.

Give.text: His shoulder servo overheats with a noise like bacon achieving liftoff.

Give.text: House rules: the overheated party forfeits the wager. The oil can is mine.

Give.text: Losing this badly took real skill. Nobody will ever believe that.

LookAt.text: He's taking notes. I'm in the notes.

LookAt.text: A round little bot built entirely out of other bots, sorting screws by mood.

LookAt.text: The stall out front says BACK IN FIVE OIL CHANGES. This is where he goes when he's back.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Rivet's the name, he says. Knows everything, sells most of it. Today only: the welcome bundle, three facts, free.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The funicular to Midtown takes ten bolts. Exact change. Gates can count -- it's all they do.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Fact two, on the house, because I look like rain: the Dynamo is winding down, whatever the posters say.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The Order that built it left three wind-up keys behind. All three together could wind the city back up. Or down.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Fact three: the harbor keeps everything this town drops, and that dumpster is -- legally -- his.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Nine bolts, I tell him. He appraises my pockets from four feet away. Correctly.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He proposes a riddle duel. His bolt against my official city notice. He collects municipal lies. Complete sets matter.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Rivet goes first: 'What has a hundred teeth and never bites?' A gear. In this town it's always a gear.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: My turn. One riddle. Alley rules.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'What winds down... but never winds up?'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He opens his mouth. He closes it. He looks uptown, at the Dynamo District.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He pays the bolt without a word.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'What's the loudest part of a foghorn?' He doesn't blink: 'The regret.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Correct, somehow. He offers a rematch. He's enjoying this.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'How many bolts make a fortune?' He counts my pockets from across the alley: 'Nine short of one.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Correct, insultingly. Rematch on the house.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He won't say how many I'm short. Alley rules: the gate breaks its own news.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He duels for stakes, and the stake he wants is paper: that official notice pinned up on the docks.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He duels somebodies only, alley rules. A somebody, here, owns something with an Order's stencil on it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says everything a bot needs is in that dumpster, and he says it as the dumpster's legal owner.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He eyes the magnet. He says the docks have a drain, the drain has a glint, and the glint has my name on it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He waves. He's already sorting the bolts I fed the gate. I'm not asking how.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He nods at the gate. 'Ten bolts, exact. Go on up,' he says. 'Wave to me from the future.'

Give.text: 'The dumpster. Mine, legally. Help yourself before the duck does. Deposit refunded.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'The storm drain on the docks. Bring the string. Deposit refunded.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'You've already withdrawn. The bank is closed. Deposit refunded.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He bites one of the nine and hands the fistful straight back, with a fact:
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'The gate. Let it count. It lives for this.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'Stakes, riddle, notary. Talk to me when you're holding paper worth wanting.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'You've got the paper AND my bolt. The shop is closed to you, champ.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He won't appraise his own bolt. Conflict of interest. The fact comes free:

Give.text: 'Ten go in the gate. All at once. It likes the drama.'

Give.text: He won't take it as a gift. Stakes or nothing -- collection ethics.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Riddles, he says, are the alley's notary.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He appraises it, names a price, and the price is an insult. We're both happier keeping it.

LookAt.text: Talent night. Tonight. No pressure. The marquee is the pressure.

LookAt.text: SPROCKET, still in lights. Winner, robbed mid-bow. The marquee doesn't mention that part.

LookAt.text: The Grand Cog Theater. TALENT NITE, says the marquee, with a line left blank underneath.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A blank line on a marquee is the most dangerous sentence in show business.

Open.text: The doors open for performers. I'm a spectator with a forearm-sized key. The doors can tell.

Smell.text: Velvet, valve oil, and flop sweat. Showbiz.

LookAt.text: A box office with a clerk built into it. Literally. He IS the booth from the waist down.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Talent night, tonight, he says. Grand prize: an oil voucher and a backstage pass.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Backstage. Where Madame Voltina reads fortunes. And guards... things. He doesn't say what things.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Sign-up needs a stage name and an act. I have a name, and a key the size of my forearm.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says, quote, that's more act than most.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: You keep your props, he adds, before I can ask. House insurance won't survive a second Houdini.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He taps the sign-up slot and looks at my key. The slot is key-shaped, conceptually.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says doors at eight, performers at seven, panicking at six. I'm ahead of schedule.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says I'm the first winner in Grand Cog history to leave lighter than I arrived.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: I pitch the act: THE AMAZING SPROCKET WINDS HIMSELF UP. With a real key. On stage.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The clerk has seen everything twice, and he still blinks.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: My name goes up on the marquee while I'm still holding the pen.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: I keep the key. It's the act.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He slides the key back. Pitches come after terms, he says. Talk first.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Already registered. One act per bot. House rules, and the house is a cog.

LookAt.text: Every bot on this island runs on that hum. Including me. The hum is flat by half a step.

LookAt.text: Half a step doesn't sound like much. Neither did the clunk.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Two rows of regulars with their arms crossed. Some of them welded that way.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Front row: a juggler rehearsing a drop, and a singer warming up a voice like a ferry in distress.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: My public. An hour ago they were a firing squad.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I try some crowd work. How's everybody doing tonight?
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: A voice from the dark: 'WORSE NOW.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Somebody yells ENCORE. Somebody else yells at him. The arts.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Idling engines. The whole room is holding its breath with fans.

LookAt.text: The catwalk is empty now. It looks innocent. Catwalks always do.

LookAt.text: The emcee. Tuxedo paint job, telescoping mic arm, and the smile of a bot who has introduced ten thousand acts and survived nine.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Sprocket, he says, reading the marquee off my face. The wind-up act. You're on next.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: One problem: the brownout caught the spotlight operator mid-juggler. He's down to a polite ember.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: House rule: no spot, no act. He says it like the rule has a plaque somewhere.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He looks from my key to the dark booth and back. He likes my chances, he says. Conditionally.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He points his mic arm at the booth. No spot, no act. The plaque is implied.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He sweeps an arm at the stage. The stage is all mine, he says, the way doctors say 'this won't hurt'.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He keeps calling me 'the discovery of the season'. The season is one night long.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Pomade. He's a robot. I have questions.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Confetti on the boards and a spotlight with nothing left to prove.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The stage. Tonight it gets a maintenance-bot and a very large key.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The key is the act. The maintenance-bot is the optimism.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The curtain opens. The house goes quiet the way a dropped wrench goes quiet. Mid-air.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The emcee leans in: how many turns? The manual mentions a number. In bold. In red.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: I wind myself. Eight full turns. Regulation maximum is five.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The spring lets go.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: I am now performing unscheduled acrobatics.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The proscenium and I meet several times. It gives as good as it gets.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: I land in the spotlight. On my feet. Nobody is more surprised than the feet.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Silence. Then the house comes apart. The bots who can't stand wave their wheels.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The emcee declares it the most honest act in Grand Cog history. I don't correct him.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: First prize: one oil voucher, and a backstage pass on a lanyard of bicycle chain.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The spring spat the key to the boards at launch. I go to collect it for the bow -- my hand closes on applause.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: A hook on a line whips up past the proscenium, and is gone.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The hatch clicks shut. Then, from the far side, knuckles: two. One. Two. They sign their work.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The hat from the card table. The spyglass from the catwalk.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Everyone in this house saw a triumph. I saw a hatch.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Three turns buys one hop and a curtsy. The house files it under 'juggler'.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The emcee mouths: the bold red number.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Five regulation turns: a clean cartwheel, a safe landing, a smattering.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'Safe,' says the emcee, the word landing like a coat-check ticket. The Grand Cog doesn't pay out for safe.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: One act per night. The house couldn't take a second one. Neither could the proscenium.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: No spot, no act. The rule has a plaque, the plaque has a lawyer.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: The spot has an operator. The operator is asleep.
Give.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: There's no crystal ball on the table. She's the ball.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Sprocket,' says the parlor. 'Late of the docks, lately of the stage.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask how she follows my career. 'The theater is wired. I am the wiring.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The coil drinks mains. The bot under the shawl is wound like the rest of us. The readings are the expensive part.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'You came for the one in the jar.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: There's a jar. In it: a brass key the size of my forearm.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The stencil on the glass matches the crate my luck came in.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'The Order left it with me. I vet the bearer.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask what the vetting costs. 'A reading. Yours.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Used to be I'd read you cold. The brownouts ended cold reads.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Three cards. Past, present, future. Answer each with what you carry.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Carried things remember. Lie, and the coil bills you.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: A card snaps onto the felt. A woodcut of a printing press, smiling.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'The past,' she says. 'Place the lie this city printed you.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Ozone at a concentration my manual files under 'leave'.

LookAt.text: Card one, face up: a printing press, smiling. The felt waits.

LookAt.text: Card two, face up: an open hand, palm up. Municipal.

LookAt.text: Card three, face down. The first two didn't do that.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Three cards spent, one key paid. The felt looks satisfied. For felt.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Green felt, scorch marks in threes. The scorches are tidy. The felt has accepted its life.

Give.text: 'You pried it off a public board. Past, verified.'

Give.text: She slides it back across the felt. The past is mine to keep.

Give.text: 'You earned that an hour ago. The past takes longer to print.'

Give.text: 'Square winnings. A pass is permission, not debt.'

Use.text: 'The Rustlers don't want a dark city. They want one that pays to stay lit.'

Use.text: 'They don't wind a thing. The city winds down for free. They mean to own the rewind.' Their words, says the card.

Use.text: Piracy, with invoices.

Use.text: 'Your future knocks,' she says. 'Twice. Once. Twice.'

Use.text: Twice. Once. Twice. I file the rhythm somewhere fireproof.

Use.text: 'It answers when they want something walking through it,' she adds. 'They will.'

Use.text: 'The reading is paid,' she says. 'Ground yourself.'

Use.text: 'Key the second,' she says. 'Lose it slower.'

TalkTo.text: 'Derrick Nine,' he says. 'A confident year.'

TalkTo.text: I ask if anyone in this room could tell it from the house pour.

TalkTo.text: 'No.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He pours it back. Gently. So the bottle doesn't hear.

TalkTo.text: I ask about the old days. This bar oiled the Great Dynamo, he says. Two hundred years. By the barrel. By appointment.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Never late. Never tipped. Never complained.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Then, last spring, it stopped. He won't say more. Grief, or a nondisclosure.

TalkTo.text: The towel stops moving.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He looks at me the way appraisers look at paperwork that might be real.

TalkTo.text: The aide takes one nose of it and slides it back, untouched. 'Too educated.'

TalkTo.text: 'Refused,' the sommelier says. 'Derrick Nine, refused. I'll be closing early.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'He has one,' the sommelier says. 'He hasn't touched it in an hour.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The glass is full. The glass has been full all night.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'That account is closed,' he says.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Then it can stand one last round, I say. For two hundred years of never complaining.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: '...Decanted under protest.'

TalkTo.text: He folds the towel like a flag and opens the floor.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The cellar takes him. The bar is suddenly very unattended.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Tabs here are inherited, not opened.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I have no ancestors. It's a known gap.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'City Hall settles in banners now,' he says. 'We have enough banners.'

TalkTo.text: The booth in the corner drinks to that without hearing it.

TalkTo.text: From under the floor, the sound of a vintage being forgiven.

TalkTo.text: He's re-alphabetizing the derricks, west to east. The evening took something out of him.

TalkTo.text: He asks what the bot at the end is having. Nothing, currently. It worries us both.

LookAt.text: Working oil in an uptown glass. He looks like a bot whose feet finally reach the floor.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A City Hall aide, off the clock. The crest on his chest is fresher than the chassis under it.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: He has the house pour and isn't drinking it. His eye keeps sliding to the dark end of the shelf.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: I slide the official notice along the bar. NOTHING IS WRONG.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He goes still the way filing cabinets are still.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'Put it away. Walls have aides.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He drains half the house pour anyway. The half he's been avoiding all night.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: 'I never saw that.'
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He says it with his eyes shut.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He won't take anything with a paper trail. Filing it would be a confession.
Give.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: I slide the official notice along the bar. NOTHING IS WRONG.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: He goes still the way filing cabinets are still.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: 'Put it away. Walls have aides.'
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: He drains half the house pour anyway. The half he's been avoiding all night.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: He won't take anything with a paper trail. Filing it would be a confession.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Primer, and under it, salt. Harbor primer.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A corner booth of City Hall aides, drinking in formation.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Every chassis the same civic grey. The paint chart probably calls it Plausible.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I lean on the rail nearby. Casually. The lean has become a discipline.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: They're toasting the new banner. The big one. Readable from the harbor.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: A young one asks which line item the banners come out of. The booth goes quiet on schedule.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: 'Civic reassurance.'
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: They drink at once, in formation. The question retires.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: They've moved on to whether NOTHING IS WRONG needs a comma. Careers are riding on the comma.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Civic grey doesn't smell. It's in the spec.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Mayor Piston, in oils. Recently enlarged.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The frame didn't keep up. He continues some distance onto the wall.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It's bolted to the wall. The wall seems resigned.
Use.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: It's bolted to the wall. The wall seems resigned.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Oil paint. The one oil in the building nobody will pour.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A floor hatch behind the bar, brass-hinged. The cellar: where oil goes to think.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: Occupied by management. I'd be interrupting a communion.
Open.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: Staff only. The hinges would report me.
Open.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Occupied by management. I'd be interrupting a communion.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Staff only. The hinges would report me.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Cool air through the seams. Down there it's still last century.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Caliper. The aide who rations doors by the degree.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: His lapel pin is a protractor. Of course it is.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He's drafting the next notice. The word NOTHING is doing a lot of work in it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask how a bot ends up dispensing angles for a living.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Doors are budget items, he says. Every degree has a cost center.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask about the Mayor. He says the Mayor has never lost an argument.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Caliper, evenly: arguments need a second participant.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: His protractor pin catches the light.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He's seen it. Eight degrees' worth.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He looks at the signature, then at the middle distance, where his career is.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He files it under RETURNED, UNFILED, and returns it.
Give.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: He's seen it. Eight degrees' worth.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: He looks at the signature, then at the middle distance, where his career is.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.text: He files it under RETURNED, UNFILED, and returns it.
UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Ink pads. Three colors. All of them red.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A desk defended by rubber stamps. RECEIVED. DECLINED. DECLINED HARDER.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I reach for a stamp. Caliper produces a form for requesting forms.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Endorsing ink. The desk has opinions it's allowed to publish.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Mayors Piston the First through Fourth. The same boiler, different dents.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Four portraits, one paint job between them. Municipal continuity.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: Bolted to the wall. Somebody anticipated me, four mayors ago.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Bolted to the wall. Somebody anticipated me, four mayors ago.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Varnish. Recent. History gets refreshed here on a schedule.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Spare banners, rolled and racked. NOTHING IS WRONG, in every size down to lapel.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: There's a fresh crate of them. Confidence has a reorder point.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

PickUp.text: The small ones are lapel-sized. My lapel declines.
PickUp.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I don't need a banner. I'm carrying the rebuttal.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Fresh ink. The same suspicious vintage as my notice.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The Dynamo fills the window. From this office you can't not see it.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: One window, floor to ceiling, curtained shut. It faces the Dynamo District.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Dust on the velvet. None on the pull cord. Somebody checks, then closes them again.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: It's seen. That was the whole job.
Open.speaker: sprocket

Open.text: I pull the cord. The Dynamo, up the hill, framed like a portrait nobody commissioned.
Open.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: It's seen. That was the whole job.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: I pull the cord. The Dynamo, up the hill, framed like a portrait nobody commissioned.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Velvet. Underneath it, ozone off the hill.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Relabeled in pencil: RESERVE. The needle has stopped pretending.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A brass gauge on the desk, labeled CIVIC OPTIMISM. The needle says plenty.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The dial behind the label is graduated in mainspring turns.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: Wired through the desk and into the Mayor's chair.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Move.text: Wired through the desk and into the Mayor's chair.
Move.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Brass polish and pencil shavings.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: A desk you could dock a ferry at. One blotter, one stamp, one crank dock on the side.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The crank handle is worn shiny.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

Use.text: The stamp says VOID. A whole desk, organized around one word.
Use.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Warm metal. The desk sits downwind of a boiler.
Smell.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: He's down to working pressure. It suits him.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: Mayor Piston. A boiler in a sash. The pressure does his gesturing for him.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

LookAt.text: The hum winds him like everybody. The steam is just the oratory.
LookAt.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He's mid-speech when I reach the desk. There's no audience. He doesn't need one.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He greets me: ah, the talent. Tremendous act. The city thanks me.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I mention the robbery. He calls that 'the magic of live theater'.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I put it to him straight: the Dynamo is dying and the city is lying.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Nothing is wrong, he says. It's on every banner.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He asks, off the record, how the maintenance business is.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Booming, I tell him. Also off the record.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I unfold the official notice on his blotter. NOTHING IS WRONG. His signature.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Standard reassurance, he says. We issue them on a schedule.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask what the schedule reassures us about, if nothing is wrong.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Steam escapes from somewhere official.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Caliper, he says. The stamp.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He stamps my notice VOID, with feeling. Now it's official twice.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Maintenance is budgeted at zero, he adds. Because nothing needs maintaining.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I tell him the hum is flat. By half a step.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Retuned, he says. By request. The city prefers a softer key.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The city is built in C. I let it go. For now.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I cite my source: Rivet. Knows everything, sells most of it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Rivet, he says, once sold this office its own staplers.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I don't doubt it. I still don't doubt him either.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I withdraw to regroup. The banners watch me go.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I lay the Oil Bar tab on the blotter, next to my voided notice.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Four rounds of single-origin crude. Charged to DYNAMO MAINT. -- CONTINGENCY. Signed: Caliper.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Caliper offers to explain the signature.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He cannot, the Mayor says. It's a very good signature.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The boiler drops a register. The room gets one degree more honest.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The Dynamo is fine, he says. I have never felt better.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I mention the curtains. Dust on the velvet, none on the cord.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He enjoys the view, he says. Briefly. In private.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: That one was true. I bank it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Zero, I observe, is a very round number for a machine that size.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Zero, he says, is the most honest number we publish.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I believe that more than he wants me to.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I withdraw to regroup. The banners watch me go.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I say nothing. I'm a maintenance-bot. We're comfortable around things that wind down.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The needle does the talking.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Oh, hang the banners, he says.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He docks the crank and winds himself, right there, in front of a maintenance-bot.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Eight turns. The maximum is five. He signs the maximums.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: It's been sagging since spring, he says. The engineers gave it three days.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: That was two days ago.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I ask about the Order. The bots who built the thing.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Wound down, he says. One by one. Pensioned off the books.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: One name left on the list. Deliveries go up the hill.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Up the hill is the Dynamo District. Behind the fence with opinions.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Then he opens a drawer and slides a letter across. Crumpled and uncrumpled. Repeatedly.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: WINDING IS A SERVICE NOW, it says. WE HAVE THE FIRST KEY. RATES ON REQUEST.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Terms of contact: the Scrap & Barrel back door. Knock twice, once, twice.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I know that knock. I've been on the wrong side of it twice.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The city cannot pay, he says.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The city cannot be seen refusing to pay.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I, fortunately, am not the city.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I get the letter, the knock, and the deniability. The city gets to be fine.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: Nothing is wrong, son, he says. Go fix it.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I offer a courtesy wind. Free estimate. No appointment necessary.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: The Mayor does not receive maintenance during office hours.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He says it with his hand still on the crank. We both notice.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He finds one more pound of pressure somewhere.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I point at the gauge. The needle, the pencil line, the math.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: That gauge measures optimism, he says. Optimism varies.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: He turns it face-down. The needle scrapes the blotter on the way.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

TalkTo.text: I step out. Behind me, a ratchet, briskly. We'll both pretend otherwise.
TalkTo.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Already stamped. The desk only escalates to ignoring.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He waves it at the visitor's chair. Appointments are conducted seated.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Fine. We'll do this with words first.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: Paper goes through channels. The channel is me, talking. So: talking.
Give.speaker: sprocket

Give.text: He takes nothing by hand. Hands are for gesturing.
Give.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

UsedWith.speaker: sprocket

Smell.text: Coal smoke. He precedes himself.
Smell.speaker: sprocket
